compositionDirection: First create an in-flow local composition draft that uses the actual proposal copy with grey placeholder frames. Default to a single body-width image or simple video. Use 16:9 by default, with 4:3, 7:5, or 5:7 only when the surrounding context asks for it. Teaspressa images can work as loose atmosphere; they do not need to prove the adjacent sentence.

defaultFrame.note: Use alternate layouts such as grids, diptychs, or tall paired screenshots only when Noah asks for them or the proposal clearly needs them.

draftPlacements.placement: After Finding 01 in the Summary tab.
draftPlacements.placeholderCopy: Selected Are.na image currently previewed in a 16:9 CSS crop.

draftPlacements.placement: After Finding 03 in the Summary tab.
draftPlacements.placeholderCopy: Selected Are.na image currently previewed in a 16:9 CSS crop.

draftPlacements.placement: Inside What's working after the merchandising paragraph.
draftPlacements.placeholderCopy: Selected Are.na image currently previewed in a 16:9 CSS crop.

draftPlacements.placement: Research tab, after the Product catalog table and the paragraph about 152 products and seasonal collections. Live in proposals/teaspressa/index.html.
draftPlacements.placeholderCopy: Edge-to-edge collage of 120 A-clean/B-usable product images, sorted by product family with narrow stick and mini cube packs in the top rows.
draftPlacements.cropRule: Tight square crop around detected product/object; no gutters, strokes, or borders.
draftPlacements.filters: A-clean/B-usable buckets only; removed sample/T-logo placeholders, obvious accessories, glassware, signage, and display fixtures; dropped three lowest-scored remaining product images to make a 15x8 grid.
draftPlacements.sortRule: Rows 1-2: narrow stick and mini cube formats. Row 3: tea line and sampler. Rows 4-7: kits, gifts, and boxed collections. Row 8: tallboys.

draftPlacements.placement: Scope tab, after Area 1: Wholesale buyer tools.
draftPlacements.placeholderCopy: Line sheet, buyer guide, reorder tool, seasonal wholesale preview, or retail catalog reference.

slots.name: Product world opener
slots.placementAnchor: After the opening company paragraph and before the first stat row, or after the stat row if the numbers should land first.
slots.compositionMove: Specimen grid or quiet evidence plate
slots.assetNeed: Three to six images that make the Luxe Mixer Cube system immediately legible: product, packaging, drink use, retail display, or seasonal collection.
selectionCriteria: Shows what Teaspressa sells without needing explanation
Feels like product system, not generic lifestyle
Can sit quietly under the proposal typography
Avoids images that are too polished compared with the rest of the page
avoid: Founder portraits
Stock-feeling cocktail imagery
Images where the product is not visible
slots.aspectPreference: Square or 4:5 images work best if this becomes a specimen grid.

slots.name: Wholesale buyer path
slots.section: summary finding 01, research wholesale, scope area 1
slots.placementAnchor: After Finding 01 in Summary, with possible reuse or expansion in Scope Area 1.
slots.assetNeed: Screenshots or collected images showing the wholesale store, login gate, case-pack product view, or any buyer-facing wholesale material.
selectionCriteria: Makes the wholesale experience visible
Shows the login-gated or case-pack structure clearly
Supports the line-sheet and reorder-tool observation
Frames friction without making the client look careless
avoid: Beautiful product images that do not show wholesale flow
Screenshots with unreadable text
Over-cropped fragments with no browser context
slots.aspectPreference: Default 16:9. Use a proof screenshot only if it feels stronger than atmosphere.
slots.currentUse: selected Are.na image in local in-flow draft; final image not inserted into live proposal

slots.name: Analytics stack diagram
slots.section: summary finding 02, research technology, scope area 3
slots.placementAnchor: After Finding 02 in Summary or at the start of Scope Area 3.
slots.assetNeed: No external image required. Generate a text-first diagram from the six tools: GA4, Hotjar, Clarity, Facebook Pixel, Klaviyo, Postscript.
selectionCriteria: Shows six tools collecting independently
Clarifies why a shared data layer matters
Uses words and lines, not logos unless logos add real clarity
avoid: Logo cloud
Generic funnel diagram
Complex architecture diagram that exceeds the claim

slots.name: Empty subscription infrastructure
slots.assetNeed: Screenshot of the empty subscription page and, if helpful, a related seasonal product or collection image that shows why subscription could make sense.
selectionCriteria: Shows absence clearly
Keeps the observation factual and neutral
Makes the subscription claim concrete
Can pair absence with seasonal product potential if needed
avoid: Mocked subscription design before the idea is approved
Images that imply Teaspressa has a current subscription offer if it does not
slots.aspectPreference: Default 16:9. Seasonal atmosphere is acceptable; empty-page proof can replace it.
slots.currentUse: selected Are.na image in local in-flow draft; final image not inserted into live proposal

slots.name: TikTok effort versus traction
slots.section: summary finding 04, research content and social
slots.placementAnchor: After Finding 04 in Summary or inside Research under Content and social.
slots.compositionMove: Evidence plate or quiet stat field
slots.assetNeed: TikTok profile screenshot, representative video grid, or three thumbnails that show production volume and hook pattern.
selectionCriteria: Shows volume of effort
Does not ridicule performance
Lets the 475 videos to 5,181 followers claim feel grounded
Can be read quickly
avoid: Selecting individual videos only because they look bad
Screenshots that make the critique feel petty
Video embeds unless a specific clip demonstrates the pattern
slots.aspectPreference: Tall mobile screenshot or three-square thumbnail strip.

slots.name: Wedding and corporate inquiry flow
slots.placementAnchor: After Finding 05 in Summary or inside Scope Area 2.
slots.assetNeed: Screenshots of Wedding Shop and corporate gifting pages, plus any imagery that shows custom kits, gifting, or event use.
selectionCriteria: Shows why the flow currently becomes manual
Connects custom offer to higher-order buyer value
Makes the quote process visible without overdesigning a new one
avoid: Wedding mood imagery with no connection to the page flow
Full custom quoting UI mockup unless explicitly approved
slots.aspectPreference: Two-page diptych or three-step process strip.

slots.name: Merchandising strength
slots.section: summary what's working, research product catalog
slots.placementAnchor: Inside What's working, after the paragraph on 100 plus collections and seasonal rotation.
slots.assetNeed: Six product, collection, retail, or seasonal images that demonstrate the sophistication of Teaspressa's merchandising system.
selectionCriteria: Shows breadth without feeling random
Each image can represent a distinct merchandising logic: drink type, season, occasion, gift, retail, flavor
Makes the proposal feel observant before it becomes advisory
avoid: Six images that all communicate the same thing
Images that are pretty but do not reveal the merchandising system
slots.aspectPreference: Current local draft uses 16:9 CSS crop. Use 4:3 or 16:9 depending on mobile review.
slots.currentUse: selected Are.na image in local in-flow draft; final image not inserted into live proposal

slots.name: Wholesale buyer tool artifact
slots.placementAnchor: Scope tab, after Area 1: Wholesale buyer tools.
slots.assetNeed: Reference images for strong wholesale line sheets, retail buyer tools, mobile reorder experiences, or campaign catalog pages. This can become a restrained mock artifact later.
selectionCriteria: Shows the kind of buyer-facing clarity we mean
Feels useful, not decorative
Can inspire a Teaspressa-specific artifact without copying another brand
avoid: SaaS dashboard references
Generic ecommerce screenshots
Overly designed line sheets that would not fit the WARWCO proposal register
slots.aspectPreference: Default 16:9. Use a document-like reference crop only if it still reads at proposal text width.
slots.currentUse: in-flow local placeholder; final image not inserted yet

slots.name: Seasonal subscription possibility
slots.placementAnchor: After Scope Area 2, if recurring revenue needs a concrete visual object.
slots.assetNeed: Images that show seasonal rotation, limited flavors, subscription boxes, or recurring product rituals. Use only if it adds clarity beyond TS-04.
selectionCriteria: Makes the seasonal subscription logic visible
Does not imply final packaging or offer terms
Adds possibility after the proof of empty infrastructure
avoid: Generic subscription box inspiration
Images that pull the proposal into a consumer moodboard
slots.aspectPreference: Square or document-like, depending on selected asset.

slots.name: Connected measurement path
slots.placementAnchor: After Scope Area 3 or before What a conversation would look like.
slots.assetNeed: No external image required. Generate a diagram showing independent tools becoming a shared measurement layer.
selectionCriteria: Clarifies what connected measurement means
Avoids pretending we know their internal data model
Gives the scope area a concrete shape
avoid: Overly technical implementation diagram
Fake data dashboard

slots.name: Research catalog breath
slots.placementAnchor: Research tab, after the Product catalog table and the paragraph about 152 products and seasonal collections.
slots.assetNeed: Review in production after deploy. If it feels too dense on mobile, create a smaller variant or a wide-only treatment.
selectionCriteria: Shows catalog breadth or product world without requiring explanation
Breaks up the table-heavy Research tab
Feels tied to product range rather than generic lifestyle
Works as a single 16:9 image at proposal text width
avoid: Another image that repeats an existing Summary image
Tiny product details that do not read at text width
Overly decorative campaign images that distract from the research flow
slots.aspectPreference: Current local draft uses a 15:8 frame matching a 15-column by 8-row square-tile collage.
slots.currentUse: Live in the Research tab after Product catalog as a 120-image product-only edge-to-edge wholesale product collage sorted by product family.
slots.cropRule: Tight square crop around detected product/object; no gutters, strokes, or borders.
slots.filters: A-clean/B-usable buckets only; removed sample/T-logo placeholders, obvious accessories, glassware, signage, and display fixtures; dropped three lowest-scored remaining product images to make a 15x8 grid.
slots.sortRule: Rows 1-2: narrow stick and mini cube formats. Row 3: tea line and sampler. Rows 4-7: kits, gifts, and boxed collections. Row 8: tallboys.
